question.label: Friction scan
question.name: What's been slower or harder than it should have been?
question.description: Invites them to name blockers without framing it as complaint.

answer: It took me a long time to kick a ball after i ate too much chocolate last week
note: He described a concrete physical slowdown after eating too much chocolate, which lightly suggests wellbeing/capacity, while the answer did not show engagement or clarity.

question.label: Opening pace check
question.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?
question.description: Opener that invites a real answer rather than 'fine'. Reads wellbeing directly.

answer: good, if i eat a lot of chocolate
note: He answered positively but lightly, with the chocolate comment suggesting decent energy without strong extra signal; no clear engagement signal emerged.

question.label: Recovery pattern
question.name: What does your recovery look like between sessions?
question.description: Gets concrete about sleep, soreness, and whether he is actually resetting.

answer: good, as long as i have a chocolate drink. but sometimes i can't find a shop to buy chocolate from.
note: He says recovery is generally good, with a specific dependency on chocolate drinks and occasional access issues, which is a mild positive with a practical sustainability wrinkle.

question.label: Match fitness load
question.name: How is your body holding up after matches and training lately?
question.description: Surfaces whether the physical load is sustainable or accumulating quietly.

note: "All good" is a neutral, non-specific answer on physical load, so it doesn't move wellbeing.

question.label: Priority clarity
question.name: What are you actually focused on this week, and why those things?
question.description: Surfaces whether priorities are crisp or vague; whether they match what you thought.

answer: finding new types of chocolate, one from every country. oh and football, i want to become the best footballer in the world. and also i want mr beast to sponsor me with his delicious chocolate bars, i will happily wear a shirt with his chocolate bars on it
note: He gave a vivid, specific set of goals with strong ownership and enthusiasm, showing crisp priorities and clear engagement.

question.label: Season target
question.name: What would make this season feel like a step forward for you?
question.description: Clarifies the outcome he is aiming for and what success means to him.

answer: if you know where i can find more chocolate, not just food or drinks but other ways to ingest chocolate
note: He answered with a chocolate-related preference rather than any season-step or development signal, so the scored axes stay neutral.

question.label: Training lift
question.name: What part of your game are you most trying to sharpen at the moment?
question.description: Surfaces current personal motivation and where he is choosing to invest effort.

answer: goalie, and between waiting for the ball to come back to me, how i can consume as much chocolate as possible
note: He named goalie as the skill he wants to sharpen, showing some growth intent, while the chocolate aside suggests the answer was only partially focused rather than strongly invested.

question.label: Feedback use
question.name: How are you using the feedback you have been getting lately?
question.description: Checks whether coaching input is being translated into action.

answer: not much, we don't talk about chocolate enough
note: He said he is using the feedback not much, which is a mild growth dip; the chocolate comment is off-topic and not scored.
